title: Bitwise Operators
content:
### Bitwise Operators

Bitwise operators work on **binary representations** of numbers. They operate bit by bit.

---

### Operator Table

| Operator | Name | Example |
|----------|------|---------|
| `&` | AND | `5 & 3` (101 & 011 = 001 = 1) |
| `|` | OR | `5 | 3` (101 | 011 = 111 = 7) |
| `^` | XOR | `5 ^ 3` (101 ^ 011 = 110 = 6) |
| `~` | NOT | `~5` (inverts all bits = -6) |
| `<<` | Left shift | `5 << 1` (1010 = 10) |
| `>>` | Right shift | `5 >> 1` (10 = 2) |

---

### Bitwise AND (`&`)

```python
# 5 = 101 (binary)
# 3 = 011 (binary)
# ---------------
# 1 = 001 (result)
print(5 & 3)  # 1
```

### Bitwise OR (`|`)

```python
print(5 | 3)  # 7
```

### Bitwise XOR (`^`)

```python
print(5 ^ 3)  # 6
```

### Bitwise NOT (`~`)

```python
print(~5)  # -6 (inverts all bits)
```

### Shift Operators

```python
print(5 << 1)  # 10 (multiply by 2)
print(5 >> 1)  # 2 (divide by 2, floor)
```

---

### Common Uses

| Use | Technique |
|-----|-----------|
| **Check if bit is set** | `x & (1 << n)` |
| **Set a bit** | `x | (1 << n)` |
| **Clear a bit** | `x & ~(1 << n)` |
| **Toggle a bit** | `x ^ (1 << n)` |
| **Check even/odd** | `x & 1` (1 = odd) |
| **Multiply/divide by 2** | `x << 1`, `x >> 1` |

---

> **Note:** Bitwise operators are useful in low-level programming, cryptography, and performance optimization. Most Python code rarely needs them.
